([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"): *Aeropuerto Internacional de Monclova*); officially *Aeropuerto Internacional Venustiano Carranza* **(Venustiano Carranza International Airport)** ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **LOV**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **MMMV**) is an international airport situated in [Frontera](/wiki/Frontera_Municipality "Frontera Municipality"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). It manages the air traffic for the city of [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), supporting domestic flights, air taxi services, flight training, and [general aviation](/wiki/General_aviation "General aviation") activities. Operated by [Administradora Coahuilense de Infraestructura y Transporte Aéreo](/wiki/Administradora_Coahuilense_de_Infraestructura_y_Transporte_A%C3%A9reo "Administradora Coahuilense de Infraestructura y Transporte Aéreo"), the airport is named in honor of the Mexican President [Venustiano Carranza](/wiki/Venustiano_Carranza "Venustiano Carranza").

["Estadística Operativa Regional"](https://www.sct.gob.mx/transporte-y-medicina-preventiva/aeronautica-civil/5-estadistica/52-estadistica-operativa-regional-traffic-statistics-by-region/). *Agencia Federal de Aviación Civil*. January 2021. Retrieved 12 February 2026.

Airport serving Monclova, Coahuila, Mexico

**Monclova International Airport** ([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"): *Aeropuerto Internacional de Monclova*); officially *Aeropuerto Internacional Venustiano Carranza* **(Venustiano Carranza International Airport)** ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **LOV**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **MMMV**) is an international airport situated in [Frontera](/wiki/Frontera_Municipality "Frontera Municipality"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). It manages the air traffic for the city of [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), supporting domestic flights, air taxi services, flight training, and [general aviation](/wiki/General_aviation "General aviation") activities. Operated by [Administradora Coahuilense de Infraestructura y Transporte Aéreo](/wiki/Administradora_Coahuilense_de_Infraestructura_y_Transporte_A%C3%A9reo "Administradora Coahuilense de Infraestructura y Transporte Aéreo"), the airport is named in honor of the Mexican President [Venustiano Carranza](/wiki/Venustiano_Carranza "Venustiano Carranza").

Over time, the airport has had service from commercial airlines, offering domestic and international flights. Until 2008, [Continental Express](/wiki/Continental_Express "Continental Express") operated a flight to [Houston](/wiki/George_Bush_Intercontinental_Airport "George Bush Intercontinental Airport"). In 2004 and from 2017 to 2018, [Aeromar](/wiki/Aeromar "Aeromar") provided a flight to [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City_International_Airport "Mexico City International Airport"). The airport handled 2,175 passengers in 2018 and 19 in 2019.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Facilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monclova_International_Airport&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Facilities")]

Monclova Airport is located at an [elevation](/wiki/Elevation "Elevation") of 568 metres (1,864 ft) above [mean sea level](/wiki/Sea_level "Sea level"), featuring two [asphalt](/wiki/Asphalt_concrete "Asphalt concrete") [runways](/wiki/Runway "Runway"). The first, designated as 06R/24L, measures 2,100 by 45 metres (6,890 by 148 ft), and the second (06L/24R) is smaller, measuring 1,458 by 17 metres (4,783 by 56 ft). The commercial aviation [apron](/wiki/Airport_apron "Airport apron") provides parking positions for one commercial aircraft, while a second apron offers parking for general aviation. Adjacent facilities include civil aviation [hangars](/wiki/Hangar "Hangar"), an [apron](/wiki/Airport_apron "Airport apron") with parking positions for [aircraft](/wiki/Aircraft "Aircraft"), and a terminal building with departure and arrival facilities for both domestic and international flights.
